messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
This is a bounce simulation (speeds differ, so the usual "pass-through" trick doesn't apply directly). Tracking events:

**Event 1 — A meets B:** A (10, +3) and B (40, −1) close a 30 cm gap at 4 cm/s → meet at **t = 7.5 s** at x = 32.5. Now A moves −3, B moves +1. C is at 65, moving −2.

**Event 2 — B meets C:** B (32.5, +1) and C (65, −2) close 32.5 cm at 3 cm/s → meet 65/6 s later, at **t = 55/3 s**, at x = 130/3. (At this exact instant A, moving left at 3 from 32.5, reaches x = 0 and falls off — 32.5/3 = 65/6 s as well.) Now B moves −1 and C moves +2; they diverge, so no more collisions.

**Falls:**
- A falls at t = 55/3 s (left end)
- C: from 130/3 to 100 at 2 cm/s → 85/3 s more → falls at t = 140/3 s (right end)
- B: from 130/3 to 0 at 1 cm/s → 130/3 s more → falls at t = 55/3 + 130/3 = **185/3 s** (left end)

The last ant (B) falls off at 185/3 ≈ 61.67 seconds.

ANSWER: 185/3
